steps.title: Identify what box plots reveal
steps.content: A box plot shows the median as the vertical line inside the box; it does not show the mean. Choices A and B make claims about means, which cannot be determined from a box plot alone, so we can immediately eliminate them.

steps.title: Read the median from each box plot
steps.content: The median is the vertical line dividing each box. For Group 1, this line sits at about 25 kg. For Group 2, this line sits at about 24 kg. Group 1's median line is positioned to the right of Group 2's median line on the mass scale.

steps.title: Compare the medians and select the correct choice
steps.content: Group 1's median ($\approx 25$ kg) is greater than Group 2's median ($\approx 24$ kg). That matches choice C — C is correct.
